chunks.content:
Article: What is that thing next to the glass of water of Reis 
[]: What is that thing next to the glass of water of Rei's ?             Quote Post by JinKazama » Thu May 19, 2005 10:25 pm I've always been wondering this, most of you probably remember there's been a couple of times you could see the close-up shots of that glass of water(looks like a half filled beaker) on the nightstand in Rei's room. And there's something next to it, as I looked more closer it seem like a paper bag of medicines, as it looks like its to be taken with water. Maybe I'm wrong but I'm just curious to know what exactly is that? As in the last episodes after Rei-03 had showed up just before the HIP took place, there was a scene of her room again, pair of broken eyeglasses and .... that glass container said earlier was in pieces too. I was wondering if this has got anything to do with the plot, e.g. Rei-03 later refused to listen to Gendou anymore, and just before that point her body started to fall apart :shock: seem like she's losing her AT field or something but then again it only happened to her didn't happen to others why? I've read some of the postings on this forum some1 suspect that it was that thing(the medicines)which helps to keep Rei's human body or would it be used for treatment of albinism. :roll: Anyway, welcome all your thoughts and imaginations its interesting to hear what others have to say. 8)

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Thu May 19, 2005 10:38 pm Are you a genie? :shock: I don't think much thought was put into those pills; most likely GAINAX simply wanted to further the "sickly girl" persona.

[]:             Quote Post by Soluzar » Thu May 19, 2005 11:45 pm Probably vitamins, or some kind of supplement. She probably doesn't eat a balanced diet. She has so little regard for her own well-being that I'd be surprised if she ate more than bare subsistence fodder, unless she is in a situation where eating is the social requirement, such as the ramen scene. If not vitamins, then possibly something for the pain? Rei is "bandage-girl", after all. She's always getting some kind of injury. "When she's not in action - she

Article: What is that thing next to the glass of water of Reis 
 the social requirement, such as the ramen scene. If not vitamins, then possibly something for the pain? Rei is "bandage-girl", after all. She's always getting some kind of injury. "When she's not in action - she's in traction! When she's not in class, she's risking her ass!"

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Fri May 20, 2005 2:04 am Ritalin, Clonazepam, Alplax, Rohipnol, Paxil.... something of that sort. Why do you think Rei is so cool and calm?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri May 20, 2005 3:29 am The drugs were not for her injuries, as she has been medicated since the days she grew up in her dungeon in Third Annex. There are a few discussions here that discuss Rei's medication. I recommend the Search function.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Fri May 20, 2005 7:10 am I have always been exceptionally curious about Rei's medication. What was it for? I wonder, could it account for her near catatonic state most of the time? Could Nerv be literally pumping her full of drugs to keep her placid and compliant. Rei can after all generate enormous AT-Fields without her Eva. Perhaps then a hefty dosage of sedatives every morning was in order, lest teenage hormones cause a Tokyo-3 destroying angst fest. Shinji: "Of course not! I can't trust a father like him!" Rei: "Grrrrrr...... Should have....taken....pillssssss....." *BBOOOOOMMMMMM!!!* Hyuuga : "The strongest A.T. Field we've ever seen!!!" On the other hand, perhaps they just supply her unique needs. Her being somewhat, different, than the rest of us.

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Fri May 20, 2005 12:22 pm Reichu wrote: The drugs were not for her injuries, as she has been medicated since the days she grew up in her dungeon in Third Annex.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Sun May 22, 2005 2:32 pm If people are drugging their normal kids to keep their behavior in check, I imagine one would want to be even more assured that their alien-god-in-a-shell will not crack under even moderate circumstances. If that were the reasoning, it might have just been better to keep her permanently sedated, stuck to a feeding tube filled with Lay's Liquid Potato Chips. "You can't suck just one."

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Sun May 22, 2005 5:48 pm My guss was that the pill wee part of RItsuko keeping her alive. With the good Doctor in lock down the treatments stoped and and hence we get the bit with the arm falling off in EoE.
